title: Server/channel customization, role presets UI, richer profiles, username autocomplete, profile popovers, and avatar upload fixes (frontend-only)

requirements.summary: Add server and channel settings UIs to view/update customizable fields (name plus additional fields when backend support is available) and reflect updated values in lists/headers.
acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user with appropriate permissions can open a server settings UI and update server fields; changes persist after refresh.
A signed-in user with appropriate permissions can open a channel settings UI and update channel fields; changes persist after refresh.
Server/channel list and relevant headers reflect updated names and images (icon/banner) when provided.

file_operations.description: Create a server settings dialog that supports editing server name via existing mutations and includes form sections for additional server fields (description/icon/banner/settings) that render only when corresponding backend data/capabilities exist. Use shadcn-ui dialog/form primitives for consistent UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a channel settings dialog that supports editing channel name via existing mutations and includes form sections for additional channel fields (topic/description/icon/settings) that render only when corresponding backend data/capabilities exist. Use shadcn-ui dialog/form primitives for consistent UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the new ServerSettingsDialog into the server list actions so authorized users can open server settings (in addition to rename/delete). Ensure the list reflects updated server name immediately after successful save via existing React Query invalidation.

Wire the new ChannelSettingsDialog into the channel list actions so authorized users can open channel settings (in addition to rename/delete). Ensure the list reflects updated channel name immediately after successful save via existing React Query invalidation.

Ensure the selected server/channel header areas (or existing UI near ChannelTimeline) display the latest server/channel names and, when available, server icon/banner and channel icon. Keep rendering resilient when these fields are not yet provided by the backend.

requirements.summary: Add a server role/rank preset UI (Owner/Admin/Moderator/Member) and use it to gate/disable server/channel management controls in the frontend, with a path for owners to customize permission flags (UI-ready for backend persistence).
acceptanceCriteria: Each server has role assignments for members and at least the preset roles available.
Only authorized roles can edit/delete/customize servers and channels; unauthorized users see disabled controls or receive a clear error.
Server owners can modify the permission flags for preset roles (customization UI + backend persistence).

file_operations.description: Create a role preset/permissions editor UI for Owner/Admin/Moderator/Member that allows an owner to view and adjust permission flags (e.g., manage server, manage channels, delete, customize). Implement as UI-first, persisting only when backend capabilities are available. Use shadcn-ui components for toggles/forms. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Integrate RolePermissionsEditor into the server settings dialog under a 'Roles & Permissions' section, showing it only for owners/admins once role data is available from the backend. Include clear English messaging when the user is not authorized to edit permissions.

Add/adjust React Query hooks needed for role/permission gating using existing backend capabilities (e.g., getCallerUserRole and isCallerAdmin). Centralize role checks and provide reusable helpers for UI gating. Mentioned selected component: Use the authorization component concepts (role-based access control + handling Unauthorized traps) to drive consistent client-side gating and error handling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace owner-only gating with role/permission-based gating for rename/delete/settings actions. Disable or hide controls for unauthorized users and show clear English feedback if backend rejects an action (trap) despite UI checks. Mentioned selected component: Use the authorization component guidance for handling authorization failures in the UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace owner-only gating with role/permission-based gating for channel rename/delete/settings actions. Disable or hide controls for unauthorized users and show clear English feedback if backend rejects an action (trap) despite UI checks. Mentioned selected component: Use the authorization component guidance for handling authorization failures in the UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Expand Profile/Settings UI to edit and save richer profile fields and render them in at least one read-only display context.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile/Settings page includes editable controls for newly added profile fields and saves them successfully.
New profile fields render in at least one read-only display context (e.g., profile preview popover and/or a profile section on the settings page).
Validation errors (if any) are shown in English and prevent invalid saves.

file_operations.description: Extend the Profile & Settings form UI to include additional editable fields (social + gamer/tag style), such as bio, links, banner, status, badges, pronouns, and theme color (or similar). Add English validation and prevent invalid saves. Keep compatibility with current backend UserProfile by conditionally including fields only when supported by returned profile shape.

Create a read-only profile fields preview component that renders the expanded profile fields (including banner/status/bio/links/etc. when present) for reuse in settings and the quick profile popover. Use shadcn-ui typography/layout primitives where appropriate. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update the initial profile setup flow to remain compatible with the expanded profile model (at minimum maintain display name + avatar), and optionally capture a minimal subset of new fields if supported, without introducing a modal flash or blocking login.

requirements.summary: Add a Profile/Settings toggle to control visibility of the user’s principal/ID to other users and enforce it across UI displays including popovers.
acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user can toggle whether their principal/ID is visible to other users and save the setting.
When ID visibility is turned off, other users do not see the principal/ID in the UI (including in popovers); the owner can still see their own ID in settings.

file_operations.description: Add a settings toggle for 'Show my ID (principal) to others' and persist it when the backend profile supports this field. Update the settings UI so the user can always see their own principal in settings regardless of the toggle, while ensuring the toggle controls visibility to other users elsewhere.

Create a small hook/utility that determines whether a viewed user's principal should be displayed based on the viewed user's saved preference and whether the viewer is the same user. Keep behavior resilient when the preference field is absent (default to hidden from others).

Ensure the quick profile preview UI (popover/modal) respects ID visibility by hiding the principal/ID when the viewed user has disabled it and the viewer is not the owner. Use shadcn-ui popover/dialog primitives. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement global username-based typing/autocomplete for user selection and identity resolution, reusing backend user search where relevant (DM start, and @-mention style selection where applicable).
acceptanceCriteria: Typing a username/display name surfaces matching users in an autocomplete dropdown with stable selection.
Selecting an autocomplete result uses the correct underlying user identity (principal/userId) for actions like starting a DM.
Backend exposes a query suitable for user search that returns consistent results for partial matches.

file_operations.description: Create a reusable username autocomplete input that queries user search and renders a keyboard-navigable dropdown of results, returning the selected Principal and display name. Use existing useSearchUsers hook and shadcn-ui input/list primitives. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Replace the current manual search results list with the reusable UserAutocompleteInput to provide stable autocomplete selection behavior and ensure the selected Principal is used for starting a DM.

Add optional @-mention style user autocomplete behavior while composing messages: when the user types a trigger (e.g., '@' + characters), surface UserAutocompleteInput-like suggestions and insert the chosen display name while preserving the underlying selected identity for future use (UI-only until backend mention support exists).
Add optional @-mention style user autocomplete behavior while composing DMs, consistent with MessageComposer (UI-only until backend mention support exists).

requirements.summary: Add a quick profile preview popover/modal from message contexts (channels and DMs) showing key profile info while respecting ID visibility settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an author avatar or display name in a message opens a profile preview UI.
The preview includes at least avatar + display name and a subset of expanded profile fields, and does not show the principal when hidden by that user.
The preview can be dismissed via click-away and/or an explicit close control.

file_operations.description: Implement the profile preview content (avatar, display name, subset of expanded profile fields via ProfileFieldsPreview) and dismissal behavior (click-away and close). Ensure it uses useGetUserProfile for the viewed user and respects ID visibility (REQ-13).

Wire ProfilePopover so clicking an author's avatar and/or display name opens the quick profile preview from channel and DM message contexts. Ensure the popover uses the author's Principal for correct profile resolution.

Optionally extend the same quick profile preview behavior to feed post author UI if present, reusing ProfilePopover for consistency and to increase read-only display coverage of expanded profile fields.

requirements.summary: Fix the profile avatar image upload flow end-to-end (settings + setup modal) with validation, progress/success feedback, persistence-friendly state, and correct display via existing avatar rendering logic.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can upload an image file as their avatar, save, refresh, and still see the uploaded avatar.
Upload failures show a clear English error message and do not corrupt the existing avatar state.
Image URL-based avatars (if present) continue to work and do not break when upload is also available.

file_operations.description: Create a shared avatar uploader component used by both ProfileSettingsPage and ProfileSetupModal that performs consistent validation, shows upload progress, and returns the uploaded Attachment for saving. Mentioned selected component: Use the blob-storage ExternalBlob capability (fromBytes + withUploadProgress + getDirectURL) to support reliable uploads and progress tracking. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace the inline avatar upload logic with AvatarUploader to ensure consistent validation/progress/success behavior. Ensure switching between URL and Upload modes does not erase the other mode’s value, and that failed uploads preserve the last known-good avatar state.

Replace the inline avatar upload logic with AvatarUploader to ensure first-time setup upload works reliably and matches the settings page behavior. Keep user-facing text in English and provide clear error messages.

Harden avatar rendering for both uploaded attachments and URL-based avatars: avoid unnecessary re-creation work across renders, handle invalid/missing data safely, and ensure fallback initials work consistently when an avatar cannot be displayed.
